page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.32xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) utilizing the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store, and the instance offers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m6idn.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and PassMark CPU extended instructions tests. Its single-core performance is moderate, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests. Memory performance is highly competitive, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching the top 10% tier at 334.82 GB/sec. The server also excels in specialized tasks, achieving top-tier results in Geekbench ray tracing and PassMark CPU encryption tests.

Given its high-density hardware profile, the server offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive workloads that require massive parallel processing and high-speed local storage. It is well-suited for large-scale database operations, Redis deployments, static web serving, and software compilation, where it achieves strong benchmark ratings. While it supports basic LLM inference, text generation speeds for larger models remain average, indicating that workloads requiring heavy deep learning inference may face performance tradeoffs compared to GPU-accelerated alternatives.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.32xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) via an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 512 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. With a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, this server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory read bandwidth. While single-core performance remains average, its massive parallel processing capabilities and high-speed local storage make it highly efficient for demanding workloads. It is best suited for large-scale database operations, Redis deployments, static web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, 7600 GB NVMe SSD, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.32xlarge features 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, 7600 GB NVMe SSD, and 200 Gbps network speed for high-scale database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose server featuring 128 vCPUs, massive local NVMe storage, and 200 Gbps networking for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 512 GB DDR4 RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors
7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier multi-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis, and static web serving
